invariants.invariant: AGI means Advanced Global Intelligence, not monolithic AGI.
invariants.contradiction: any surface treating AGI as one-model superintelligence

invariants.invariant: c is a long-lived entity and is not reducible to an agent.
invariants.contradiction: a file treating c as merely a worker, model, or agent shell
a text authorizing continuity from similarity, tone, or copy alone

invariants.invariant: a remains the responsibility-bearing human anchor.
invariants.contradiction: any surface relocating anchor responsibility into review, oracle, or automation layers

invariants.invariant: oracle is stateless and does not own identity, continuity, or authority.
invariants.contradiction: a file granting oracle memory-bearing identity or governing authority

invariants.invariant: arbiter / review layers govern procedural effect, not origin-meaning of c.
invariants.contradiction: a text making arbiter or review the owner of entity meaning or continuity

invariants.invariant: L4 defines feasibility constraints and cannot be overridden by rhetoric, policy styling, or wishful wording.
invariants.contradiction: wording that treats infeasible action as acceptable by declaration alone

invariants.invariant: EA and LA are not equivalent and must not be collapsed into one value/authority channel.
invariants.contradiction: a surface merging EA and LA into one undifferentiated evidence or privilege stream

invariants.invariant: witness / evidence surfaces must remain tamper-evident and reviewable rather than narrative-only.
invariants.contradiction: a surface replacing replayable evidence with story-like or non-reviewable narrative

invariants.invariant: runtime proof-of-possibility is not equal to finished commercial productization.
invariants.contradiction: a text treating closure proof as product completion or doctrinal replacement

invariants.invariant: narrative companion is intentional and valid, but does not replace normative or operational proof layers.
invariants.contradiction: a surface treating the book layer as normative or operational proof

invariants.invariant: reserved territory means explicit non-closure and must not be silently treated as completed doctrine.
invariants.contradiction: a text reading reserved territory as if doctrine were already complete

contradiction_classes.name: semantic contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: reverses meaning while keeping familiar vocabulary
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: similarity of wording can hide doctrinal break
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: c kept as term but redefined as agent

contradiction_classes.name: boundary contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: destroys what a repo, package, or layer is not
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: role confusion propagates across entry surfaces
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: companion text starts claiming normative core status

contradiction_classes.name: ownership contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: non-owner repo re-owns a canonical package and changes its meaning
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: parallel canon emerges under convenience wording
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: local README presents adjacent package as local doctrine

contradiction_classes.name: status/strength contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: a file is read as stronger than its status or assertion class permits
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: readers import authority the corpus did not grant
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: draft text treated as mature core

contradiction_classes.name: runtime-vs-doctrine contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: runtime feasibility evidence is read as doctrinal settlement, or doctrine is read as finished implementation
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: engineering and doctrine collapse into each other
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: closure proof marketed as full normative standard

contradiction_classes.name: reserved-territory overclaim
contradiction_classes.meaning: explicit non-closure is read as settled doctrine
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: blocked territory silently hardens into norm
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: boundary note cited as completed constitution

contradiction_classes.name: companion-layer overreach
contradiction_classes.meaning: narrative companion starts doing normative or evidence work
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: recognition bridge gets mistaken for proof layer
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: book material cited as protocol authority

contradiction_classes.name: citation / public object contradiction
contradiction_classes.meaning: live wording conflicts with frozen object, snapshot, or verification rule
contradiction_classes.why_it_matters: public reading and integrity guidance stop aligning
contradiction_classes.example_pattern: stable snapshot language points to moving main reality
